zone_6b_notes: Hardy in Zone 6b. Two types commonly grown in Missouri: European plum (Stanley, President â€” firm, prune-type; partly self-fruitful) and Japanese plum (Methley, Santa Rosa â€” juicy, sweet; needs pollinator). Stanley European plum is the most reliable self-fruitful type for Zone 6b. Japanese plums bloom earlier and are more frost-sensitive. Methley is self-fruitful and the most popular Japanese type in Missouri. Thin fruit to 3â€“4 inches apart after natural June drop for larger fruit. Plum curculio is the primary pest â€” attack begins at petal fall. Trees productive for 10â€“20 years.

planting.best_planting_time: Plant dormant bare-root trees March to early April

water: 1â€“1.5 inches/week; drought stress causes premature fruit drop
